```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(readr)
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(stringr)
```

```{r}
source("cleaning_pipeline.R")
```

Vamos a contar el numero de publishers y developers por juego para poder calcular el maximo de developers y publishers que puede llegar a tener un juego

```{r}
def_pub_dev <- df_clean %>% select(appid, publishers, developers)
def_pub_dev[def_pub_dev$appid == 298280,]$publishers = def_pub_dev[def_pub_dev$appid == 298280, ]$developers
```

```{r}
pubs_list <- unlist(def_pub_dev$publishers) %>% unique()
devs_list <- unlist(def_pub_dev$developers) %>% unique()

df_pubs_and_devs <- data.frame(companies = unlist(c(pubs_list, devs_list)) %>% unique())
```

```{r}
df_pubs_and_devs <- df_pubs_and_devs %>% mutate(
  companies = gsub("[.,()\'\"]", " ", companies),
  companies = gsub("\\s+", " ", companies),
  companies = gsub("(\\d)\\s+([^\\s])", "\\1\\2", companies),
  companies = gsub("The ", " ", companies),
  companies = gsub("Black ", " ", companies),
  companies = gsub("Team ", " ", companies),
  companies = gsub("Studio ", " ", companies),
  companies = gsub("Pixel ", " ", companies),
  companies = trimws(companies)
)
```

```{r}
splited_companies <- split(df_pubs_and_devs$companies, word(df_pubs_and_devs$companies))
```

```{r}
# Función para encontrar el prefijo común más largo en un vector de nombres
# Para poder unificar nombres de desarrolladores o publishers similares
common_root <- function(names_vec) {
  # 1. Normalizar
  clean <- tolower(names_vec)
  clean <- gsub("\\(.*?\\)", "", clean)          # quitar paréntesis
  clean <- gsub("[^a-z0-9 ]", " ", clean)        # quitar símbolos raros
  clean <- gsub("\\s+", " ", clean)              # espacios múltiples
  clean <- trimws(clean)

  # 2. Separar palabras
  words_list <- strsplit(clean, " ")

  # 3. Calcular el mínimo número de palabras
  min_len <- min(sapply(words_list, length))

  # 4. Buscar prefijo común palabra a palabra
  prefix <- c()
  for (i in seq_len(min_len)) {
    w <- sapply(words_list, function(x) x[i])
    if (length(unique(w)) == 1) {
      prefix <- c(prefix, w[1])
    } else break
  }

  # 5. Volver a capitalizar bonito
  root <- paste(prefix, collapse = " ")
  root <- tools::toTitleCase(root)
  return(root)
}
```

```{r}
generic_words <- c(
  "digital", "studio", "studios", "interactive",
  "entertainment", "software", "media",
  "big", "small", "tiny", "pixel", "pixels",
  "red", "blue", "green", "black", "white",
  "team", "dev", "developers", "productions", "the"
)


normalize_name <- function(x) {
  x <- tolower(x)
  x <- gsub("\\(.*?\\)", "", x)
  x <- gsub("[^a-z0-9 ]", " ", x)
  x <- gsub("\\s+", " ", x)
  trimws(x)
}


common_root_valid <- function(names_vec, generic_words, min_words = 1) {
  clean <- normalize_name(names_vec)
  words_list <- strsplit(clean, " ")

  min_len <- min(sapply(words_list, length))
  prefix <- character(0)

  for (i in seq_len(min_len)) {
    w <- sapply(words_list, `[`, i)
    if (length(unique(w)) == 1) {
      prefix <- c(prefix, w[1])
    } else break
  }

  if (length(prefix) < min_words) return(NULL)
  if (prefix[1] %in% generic_words) return(NULL)

  tools::toTitleCase(paste(prefix, collapse = " "))
}


add_to_groups <- function(name, groups, generic_words) {

  # Si no hay grupos aún
  if (length(groups) == 0) {
    groups[[name]] <- c(name)
    return(groups)
  }

  for (root in names(groups)) {
    candidate <- c(groups[[root]], name)

    new_root <- common_root_valid(candidate, generic_words)

    if (!is.null(new_root)) {
      # Reasignamos el grupo con la nueva raíz
      groups[[root]] <- NULL
      groups[[new_root]] <- candidate
      return(groups)
    }
  }

  # Si no encaja en ningún grupo
  groups[[name]] <- c(name)
  groups
}

```

```{r}
groups <- list()

length(df_pubs_and_devs$companies)
i <- 0
for (n in df_pubs_and_devs$companies) {
  groups <- add_to_groups(n, groups, generic_words)
  i <- i+1
  cat(i, sep = "\n")
}
```

```{r}
invert_company_map <- function(company_map) {
  inverted <- list()

  for (root in names(company_map)) {
    variants <- company_map[[root]]

    for (v in variants) {
      inverted[[v]] <- root
    }
  }

  inverted
}

# Invert de map values to complete the names simplification
company_lookup <- invert_company_map(groups)

prepare_name <- function(name) {
  normalized_name <- gsub("[\\(\\.,\\)]", "", name)
  normalized_name <- gsub("\\s+", " ", normalized_name)
  normalized_name <- trimws(normalized_name)
  normalized_name
}

normalize_company <- function(name, lookup) {
  if (is.na(name)) return(NA_character_)
  normalized_name = prepare_name(name)
  if (normalized_name %in% names(lookup)) {
    lookup[[normalized_name]]
  } else {
    normalized_name
  }
}
```

```{r}
df_pubs_devs_clean$publishers <- lapply(
  df_pubs_devs_clean$publishers,
  function(pubs) {
    unique(
      unlist(
        lapply(pubs, normalize_company, lookup = company_lookup),
        use.names = FALSE
      )
    )
  }
)

```

```{r}
df_pubs_devs_clean$developers <- lapply(
  df_pubs_devs_clean$developers,
  function(pubs) {
    unique(
      unlist(
        lapply(pubs, normalize_company, lookup = company_lookup),
        use.names = FALSE
      )
    )
  }
)
```
